paper_title: Mango Pollen Storage and Viability Study

methods.description: This method describes the collection and preparation of pollen from four mango varieties for storage and viability testing.

steps.description: The pollen used in this experiment was collected from freshly opened flowers of all four mango cultivars between 8:00 and 10:00 am (≤23 °C).
steps.searchable_summary: collect pollen from freshly opened mango flowers

steps.description: Flowers were collected prior to anthesis, a stage characterized by pink anther colour. The harvested flowers were placed in the sun to prompt anther dehiscence.
steps.searchable_summary: prompt anther dehiscence by sun exposure

steps.description: Freshly dehisced anthers, with pollen grains visible on the anthers as a greyish powder, were collected in a petri dish using forceps and dried in a desiccator for two hours.
steps.searchable_summary: dry collected anthers in a desiccator

methods.description: This method outlines the storage of pollen grains in different media and conditions to test their viability over time.

steps.description: Dried pollen grains were suspended in the different three storage media, viz. n-hexane, paraffin oil and without media and stored at room temperature in a controlled growth room (27 ºC) and at low temperatures in a cold-storage facility (4 ºC, -4 ºC, -20 ºC).
steps.searchable_summary: store pollen in various media and temperatures

steps.description: Pollen grains were separated from oil/organic solvent via a filtration process. Filter paper was also used in oil treatments to remove the paraffin oil from the pollen surface.
steps.searchable_summary: filter pollen grains from storage media

steps.description: The pollen grains on the filter paper were then rinsed 4-5 times with n-hexane to remove residual oil, before they were dried for 10-15 minutes.

methods.description: This method describes the testing of pollen viability using the aceto-carmine staining technique.

steps.description: Pollen viability was estimated using aceto-carmine test. This method includes the addition of colorant on pollen.
steps.searchable_summary: test pollen viability with aceto-carmine stain

methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the collected data using a Completely Randomized Design (Factorial).

steps.description: Statistical analysis of data of various characters was carried out as per Completely Randomized Design (Factorial).
Analysis of variance was worked out using standard statistical procedures as described by Panse and Sukhatme (1985).
steps.searchable_summary: conduct ANOVA as per Panse and Sukhatme
